title: Rhode Island HHS response

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Rhode Island HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Rhode Island Department of Health remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Rhode Island Department of Health employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: RE: 26-137: RI Access to Public Records Act Request: Rhode Island HHS response
communications.communication: The Rhode Island Department of Health (“RIDOH”) is in receipt of your request, pursuant to R.I. Gen. Laws §§ 38-2-1 et seq. (the “Access to Public Records Act”). Please be advised that, because of the number of requests pending, RIDOH requires additional time to respond to your request and, pursuant to R.I. Gen. Laws §38-2-3, RIDOH may take up to twenty (20) additional business days  to comply with your request.

communications.subject: RE: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request #26-137
communications.communication:
Ileana,
Please see attached, in response to your records request.

The records deemed public in the possession of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH) are attached hereto. We note that some of the records you requested are deemed not to be public pursuant to (i) R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2.2(4)(A)(1)(a), which protects information subject to attorney-client privilege from disclosure, and § 38-2-2(4)(K) which protects preliminary drafts, notes, impressions, memoranda, working papers, and work products.

R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-8 requires we provide you notice of your right to appeal this response to the Director of Health, Attention Legal Services, Three Capitol Hill, Providence, RI  02908, file a complaint with the Department of the Attorney General, 150 South Main Street, Providence, Rhode Island, 02903, or file a complaint with the Rhode Island Superior Court of the county where the relevant records are maintained.

Further information concerning the Access to Public Records Act is available through the Attorney General’s website at www.riag.ri.gov<http://www.riag.ri.gov>

Joseph Wendelken
Public Information Officer
Rhode Island Department of Health

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eva Tang

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #25-2869
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for furnishing the documents given so far.

On December 14, 2025, I received a communication from the City stating, "Lastly, we will not be providing records related to queries made by outside agencies using our data. For information regarding inquiries conducted by other agencies, we respectfully direct you to submit a request to those agencies directly. "

However, MVPD's policy manual section 460.9 states a few things: Outside agencies make written requests for ALPR data including their agency's name, the name of the person requesting, and the intended purpose of obtaining that information, the request must be reviewed by the Special Operations Division Captain or authorized designee, and then the request is retained on file. Given that these requests are retained on file by MVPD and are not subject to any exemptions from the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), MVPD is responsible for releasing them to requestors as per the FOIA. Please provide the responsive materials forthwith.

Respectfully,
Eva Tang

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #25-2869
communications.communication:
Hello MVPD Records Division,

On 12/14/2025, I received a message related to my records request #25-2869, stating, "Lastly, we will not be providing records related to queries made by outside agencies using our data. For information regarding inquiries conducted by other agencies, we respectfully direct you to submit a request to those agencies directly. "

On 02/12/2026, I received another message furnishing said queries, but with the entire column showing reasons for each search redacted.

California Government Code Section 6253 subdivision d item 3 states, "The notification of denial of any request for records required by Section 6255 shall set forth the names and titles or positions of each person responsible for the denial."

Please set forth the names and positions of each person responsible for withholding and redaction in each instance above.

Thank you,
Eva Tang

communications.subject: Your City of Mountain View public records request #25-2869 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Mountain View ************************************************************************ Record request #25-2869 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Requester: California Government Code Section 7922.540 requires the City to provide the names and titles or positions of each person responsible for the denial of a California Public Records Act request. The City has not denied your request for records. The City has provided all records responsive to your request. Because these are records created by other law enforcement agencies and not by the City of Mountain View, these records have been redacted by Flock to remove records of criminal investigations, intelligence information, or law enforcement security procedures pursuant to Government Code Sections 7923.600-7923.625. These records have also been redacted to remove personally identifying information pursuant to Government Code Section 7922.000. These records do contain a unique search identifier that can be used to request more specific records from the originating law enforcement agency. Your request is now closed, but will remain accessible through your NextRequest account. If you desire additional records, please submit a new request through the portal. Closed requests are not monitored. Best, Mountain View Police Department Records Division ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Mountain View.

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (District Of Columbia Board Of Elections)

communications.subject: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (District Of Columbia Board Of Elections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: Re: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (District Of Columbia Board Of Elections)
communications.communication:
​Ms. Nielsen:


I am writing to acknowledge the receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated February 19, 2026.  In accordance with D.C. Official Code § 2-532(c), I will either make the documents you have requested, or a legally segregable portion thereof, available to you, or notify you of my determination not to make the requested documents available, on or before Thursday, March 12, 2026, which is the 15th business day after the date upon which I received your request.


While FOIA requests may be made submitted by mail, fax, or email, please note that FOIA requests submitted online through the DC FOIA portal<https://myfoiadc.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/(S(siyfpx1ughr034w0udncodaq))/supporthome.aspx> will be easier to track and process.


Should you require any additional information prior to the receipt of my response, please do not hesitate to contact me.


Sincerely,

​

Terri Stroud
General Counsel
District of Columbia Board of Elections
1015 Half Street, SE
Washington, DC 20003
email: tstroud@dcboe.org
Website:www.dcboe.org
Phone: 202-727-3088

title: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (San Francisco District Attorney's office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (San Francisco District Attorney's office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) discussing procurement, purchase, installation, operating procedures, presentations, and policy relating to the use and deployment of Flock ALPRs
2) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between your office's personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase, installation, or usage of the ALPRs. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com". You may de-duplicate emails as needed in case Flock sends out newsletters or emails to multiple office members. 

In the event that you determine certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, I ask that you provide a detailed explanation of the records or portions of records being withheld and the statutory exemptions under which they are being withheld.
In the event that you determine SFPD does not handle or maintain certain records or portions of records, please indicate which office or department does.

I am requesting these records as a journalist in order to write articles in the public interest about the operations of a government agency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Austin Theriault

title: Maine DOC Youth Pay to Stay Policy

communications.subject: Maine Freedom of Access Act Request: Maine DOC Youth Pay to Stay Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maine Freedom of Access Act, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by 1 M.R.S.A. § 408(6). The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

communications.subject: **FOAA Request** 2025101 Jenny Wadhwa 27 Oct 25
communications.communication:
The Maine Department of Corrections has received your request for information under the Maine Freedom of Access Act (FOAA).  Your request will be evaluated to determine if it comes within the FOAA, if it is clear as to what information you are requesting, and if nonconfidential records already exist that would fulfill all or part of your request.  If your request does not come within the FOAA, is not clear, or no records exist, you will be so notified.  If records exist, but they are confidential, you will be notified of the basis for that determination.  If nonconfidential records exist, you will be provided a good faith, nonbinding estimate of the time within which you will be provided the records, as well as an estimate of the costs that are allowed by the FOAA, if applicable.

V/r

Jenna Heald
Maine Department of Corrections
Policy & PowerDMS Assistant
FOAA Coordinator

communications.subject: **FOAA Request** 2025100 & 2025101 Jenny Wadhwa 27 Oct 25
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,
Please see attached document responsive to both your requests.


The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of inmate fees and debt:

  1.  All current policies and procedures regarding medical copays and room & board costs or fees for which inmates are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).
Policies and Rules | Department of Corrections<https://www.maine.gov/corrections/policies>

2.12 - RESIDENT ACCOUNTS (APA RULE)<https://www.maine.gov/corrections/sites/maine.gov.corrections/files/inline-files/Policy%202.12_0.pdf>

2.12 - Attachment A - COLLECTION PRIORITY LIST<https://www.maine.gov/corrections/sites/maine.gov.corrections/files/2025-10/1025%2002.12%20attach%20a.pdf>



b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding total fees collected from inmates for medical copays and room and board fees from 2020-2024. Please include the number of individuals charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed this past calendar year. Please also include the total amount of room and board fees collected through the work-release program. Room and Board information is Attached. We do not have a report for copays.

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc). Juveniles don't have room and board obligations.

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024. MDOC does not track this information.


V/r

Jenna Heald
Maine Department of Corrections
Policy & PowerDMS Assistant
FOAA Coordinator

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Hamilton County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Hamilton County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Samuel Sinyangwe.

title: NY Crime Analysis Centers

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NY Crime Analysis Centers
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Network Overview

A current list of all Crime Analysis Centers supported or funded by DCJS, including location, host agency, and counties served by each center.
Current staffing levels at each center (by role/position, not individual names).
Annual funding allocations to each center for fiscal years 2023, 2024, and 2025.

2. Technology & Vendors

All current contracts, memoranda of understanding, and licensing agreements with technology vendors providing products or services to the CAC network. This includes but is not limited to: license plate reader systems (Flock, Vigilant, etc.), facial recognition software, social media monitoring tools, gunshot detection systems, case management platforms, and data analytics software.
A list or inventory of technology systems accessible to CAC analysts, by center.
Any promotional, demonstration, or training materials received from technology vendors.

3. Policies & Procedures

Current operational policies, standard operating procedures, and guidelines governing CAC operations statewide.
Policies governing data access, retention, and deletion for information processed by CACs.
Policies governing information sharing between CACs and external agencies, including federal agencies (ICE, HSI, CBP, FBI, ATF, DHS).
Any policies or guidance regarding CAC responses to requests from federal immigration enforcement agencies.

4. Data Sharing & Federal Coordination

Sincerely, 

EJ Fox

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Oklahoma State Election Board)

communications.subject: Oklahoma Open Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Oklahoma State Election Board)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oklahoma Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: Open Records Request
communications.communication:
Ms. Nielsen,

This is to acknowledge your letter, received March 3, 2026, in which you make an Open Records Act request, regarding the following: all memoranda of understanding, agreements, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information from January 20, 2025 to the present, including any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements, and any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.
Oklahoma’s Open Records Act, 51 O.S., §§ 24A.1 – 24A.31, requires an agency to provide “prompt, reasonable access” to records while employing reasonable procedures to protect the integrity and organization of the records and to prevent excessive disruption of the agency’s essential functions. 51 O.S., § 24A.5(6).
Our review process entails initial intake, records search, legal review, and redaction of confidential information that may be captured by the search. Further, there are many exemptions and exceptions under Oklahoma’s Open Records Act, which provide for confidentiality of specific information covered under 51 O.S. § 24A.1 et seq. If your search implicates any of these exemptions or exceptions, it may take additional time to assemble, review, and fulfill your request.
Currently, the Oklahoma State Election Board is working on a number of open records requests. We will respond to your request as quickly as possible and will notify you once our search and review is complete. Please feel free to contact me at mmohr@elections.ok.gov<mailto:mmohr@elections.ok.gov> with any questions or concerns.


Misha Mohr ​| Director of Communications and Public Information
Oklahoma State Election Board
p: 405.522.6624
Oklahoma.gov<https://oklahoma.gov/> | oklahoma.gov/elections
[306B64EC]

title: Flock Audit

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

Scott Conway

title: Request to Army for entertainment industry reports, 2019-2024

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Army for entertainment industry reports, 2019-2024
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All OCPA-LA Weekly Plans and Outreach Summaries, or equivalent reports or internal bulletins/circulars pertaining to entertainment industry collaborations, for the years 2019-2024. Attached is an example of the type of document I am seeking, except that it is for the years 2017-2018.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies," and I have used documents released to me by NASA via FOIA in published research articles. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

Hello Angie,

Thank you for your support on this request. I would like to clarify that this is the web form to use for the United States Army FOIA Requests: https://foia.army.mil/Home/FOIARequest. In addition, am I correct in concluding that my request has been forwarded to the U.S. Army FOIA Office? Does any further action need to be done on my end?

Thank You

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Army for entertainment industry reports, 2019-2024 - [UNCLASSIFIED]
communications.communication:
UNCLASSIFIED
I copied the office who originally received your request (also copied again).

After searching our tracking system, I could not find that it was referred to another office.

As a business courtesy, I did check again today to see if it had yet been referred and still do not find that it was referred to another office.

As they have been copied throughout this process, I don't think you need to do anything further until they let you know who they referred your request to.

This office would not have purview over OCPA-LA Weekly Plans and Outreach Summaries. Those records would be under the purview of the Office of the Chief of Public Affairs (OCPA). Their FOIA point of contact is: Usarmy.pentagon.hqda-ocpa.mbx.foia@army.mil<mailto:Usarmy.pentagon.hqda-ocpa.mbx.foia@army.mil>. You may wish to submit your request with them directly.

v/r,

Angie

Angela R. Keel-Welsh

communications.subject: Fw: FOIA Request Generated from Website
communications.communication:
Greetings,

You have reached the Headquarters Department of Army Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Referral Center. We pride ourselves on providing exceptional customer service to reach the highest level of customer satisfaction.

Our office is not the records owner of the documents that you are seeking. Requests for information and documents under the purview of the Army are referred to the proponent agency for appropriate handling in accordance with Army Regulation 25-55, The Army Freedom of Information Act program. Your FOIA request has been assigned a case tracking number: FA-26-1045 / FP-26-004728.

The correct office to process your request is the Office of the Chief of Public Affairs FOIA Office. Your request was referred to that office via email for processing and direct response to you.

Note: Once the appropriate office receives your request, a new FOIA case tracking number will be assigned, and their contact information is listed below.

Office of the Chief of Public Affairs
1500 Army Pentagon
 Room 1E487
Washington, DC 20310
Phone: 703-693-6578
Email: Usarmy.pentagon.hqda-ocpa.mbx.foia@army.mil

Thank you for your submission.
DA FOIA

title: NC mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: NC mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Planning documents, feasibility studies, pilot proposals, implementation roadmaps, and any vendor marketing materials or brochures provided to the agency, as well as internal deliberations about whether or how to move forward with an mDL program, created between January 1, 2023 and the present.

Agreements, contracts, or memoranda of understanding with vendors or partners, including but not limited to AAMVA, IDEMIA, Thales, GET Group, Apple, Google, Samsung, or other providers.

Interagency or interstate coordination documents, including but not limited to MOUs or correspondence with AAMVA or other states regarding mDL interoperability or standards development.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for March 8 Arrest Report of Ricky Harding, 33, Who Led Sacramento Sherriff's on Pursuit Around 9 pm (Sacramento County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I'm requesting all public records and booking images regarding the 33-year-old suspect, Ricky Harding, who was arrested at approximately 9 pm on March 8, 2026, after a pursuit that ended with the suspect crashing into a Sacramento Police vehicle.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

ROBERT RINCON

title: 4th and Maiden Flock Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: 4th and Maiden Flock Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Recordings from the Flock LPR camera located at 4th and Maiden Ln from 2/16/2026 starting at 5:00 PM lasting until 6:00 PM.  This camera is on the south west corner of the intersection facing a southern direction.  The records requested should include stills, video, and all other records generated by the camera. I request the data from Flock OS and the camera's internal storage.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.subject: Online Form Submission #23981 for Request for Public Information
communications.communication:
The City Clerk’s office is in receipt of your request.

Please note this request may take more than 72 hours to complete. You will be notified when it’s completed.

Be advised that the City of Joplin passed Resolution No. 2004-038 on the 16th day of August 2004 which pertains to charging a fee for obtaining information.  This resolution allows the City to charge for staff research time and for copying fees.

If requested, we will provide you with an estimate of charges before processing.  We do not waive fees for requests.


Holly Nagy
City Clerk
602 S Main St
Joplin, MO 64801
phone: 417-624-0820<tel:417-624-0820> Ext. 1220
email: HNagy@joplinmo.org<mailto:HNagy@joplinmo.org>
web: JoplinMo.org<https://www.joplinmo.org>
[Joplin Brand Horizontal.png]

communications.subject: Re: Online Form Submission #23981 for Request for Public Information
communications.communication:
Good morning,


A deposit of $ 23.57  is required to compile your request. If the cost exceeds $ 23.57 you will be required to make additional payment, if actual costs are less than the estimate, a refund will be issued.


Crime Analyst - 1 hr @ $23.57 = $23.57 for labor/research.



Once we receive your deposit, we will begin working on your request.



Payment can be mailed to 602 S. Main Street, Joplin, MO 64801.  Please make payment out to City of Joplin and mark the envelope attn City Clerk.



Currently, we do not accept payments by phone or online. You may also drop off your payment at the Finance Department. Take your payment to the third floor and let them know you are paying for an open records request.   We are open Monday-Friday 8AM-5PM.




Holly Nagy
City Clerk
602 S Main St
Joplin, MO 64801
phone: 417-624-0820<tel:417-624-0820> Ext. 1220
email: HNagy@joplinmo.org<mailto:HNagy@joplinmo.org>web: JoplinMo.org<https://www.joplinmo.org>

communications.subject: Online Form Submission #23981 for Request for Public Information
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Thank you for your payment.

Please see your requested information attached.

The Joplin Police Department accesses and maintains the requested camera data through its Flock portal in the ordinary course of business. The system captures still images but does not record video, so there are no video records responsive to your request.

We have provided all responsive, non‑exempt records accessible through our system. The Sunshine Law does not require the Department to obtain duplicate copies of the same data directly from the vendor or from the camera’s internal storage in addition to what we can access via our portal.

Thank you.


Holly Nagy
City Clerk
602 S Main St
Joplin, MO 64801

communications.communication: There are roughly 40 mintues where there are no recordings produced.  I would like these records as well.

communications.subject: RE: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: 4th and Maiden Flock Records
communications.communication:
Mr. Williams,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention.  We have reached out to Flock and requested their assistance, as it appears we are currently experiencing a technical issue affecting this functionality.

Our team is actively working on this with their support, and we will provide you with an update as soon as we have more information or a resolution timeline.

Thank you.

title: Request to Navy for all 2024 movie collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for all 2024 movie collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films that the U.S. Navy has participated in the production of, in any capacity whatsoever, for the year 2024. Any and all such lists, full or partial, would be appreciated and may include, but are not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports, production assistance agreements, or similar internal documents. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices, public affairs offices, and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: City of Fort Worth ChatGPT | City Manager

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: City of Fort Worth ChatGPT | City Manager
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Chat histories of all ChatGPT sessions conducted by city employees on city-owned devices or used in job-related functions in the following departments: City Manager

The timeframe for this request is 7/1/2025 to the date this request is processed. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Please make records available in installments as they are ready to release.

If it’s helpful, please share with respondents the following help article and instructions for exporting ChatGPT histories into a .zip folder:

https://help.openai.com/en/articles/7260999-how-do-i-export-my-chatgpt-history-and-data

1. Sign in to ChatGPT.
2. In the top right corner of the page click on your profile icon.
3. Click Settings.
4. Click Data Controls menu.
5. Under Export Data click Export.
6. In the confirmation screen click Confirm export.
7. You should receive an email with your data.
Note: The link in the email expires after 24 hours
8. Click Download data export to download a .zip file will be downloaded including your chat history in chat.html as well as your other data on file with us

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bradford William Davis

communications.subject: Open Records Request :: R000736-102925
communications.communication:
Dear Bradford  Davis: 
 Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of Fort Worth, Texas. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act. Your request was received in this office and given the log number R000736-102925 for tracking purposes. 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant City department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 

 
Again, thank you for using the Open Records Center. 
 Public Information Administrator 
 
 This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored email account. Please DO NOT REPLY

RE: Public Information Request R000736-102925 

Dear Bradford  Davis, 

This letter responds to your request for information to the City of Fort Worth, dated and received in our office on 10/29/2025 9:31:16 PM. 

Information Requested: - To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Chat histories of all ChatGPT sessions conducted by city employees on city-owned devices or used in job-related functions in the following departments: City Manager 

The timeframe for this request is 7/1/2025 to the date this request is processed. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Please make records available in installments as they are ready to release. 

If it’s helpful, please share with respondents the following help article and instructions for exporting ChatGPT histories into a .zip folder: 

https://help.openai.com/en/articles/7260999-how-do-i-export-my-chatgpt-history-and-data 

1. Sign in to ChatGPT. 
2. In the top right corner of the page click on your profile icon. 
3. Click Settings. 
4. Click Data Controls menu. 
5. Under Export Data click Export. 
6. In the confirmation screen click Confirm export. 
7. You should receive an email with your data. 
Note: The link in the email expires after 24 hours 
8. Click Download data export to download a .zip file will be downloaded including your chat history in chat.html as well as your other data on file with us 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Bradford William Davis 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2117891/ 

The City of Fort Worth has reviewed its files and has located the enclosed documents that are responsive to your request. Although the Public Information Act allows a governmental body to charge for copying documents, the enclosed is being provided to you at no charge. 

If you have any questions, or wish to discuss this further, you may contact me at 817-392-6112. 

Sincerely, 

Victoria Esquivel 
City Manager's Office

communications.subject: University of South Carolina -  Records Request FR-2025-519 Fee Required
communications.communication:
Dear Kelly North, 
  
 
This is in response to the Freedom of Information Act request that you recently directed to the University of South Carolina. The University has determined that it possesses documents that are responsive to your request. However, the University anticipates that some of the responsive documents, or portions of them, may be exempt from disclosure under the Act. 
  
 The Freedom of Information Act authorizes the University to collect fees not to exceed the actual cost of searching for, retrieving, redacting, and making copies of the records you have requested. The Act also authorizes the University to require you to post a deposit of 25% of the estimated costs before searching for or making copies of the records. 
  
 The University estimates that the total cost it will incur to respond to your request will be $12.50. Therefore, you must post a deposit of at least $3.13 before it gathers the records you requested. If you would like to proceed with your request in its current form, then please forward a check payable to “University of South Carolina” to me at your earliest convenience. The University will make the non-exempt documents that are responsive to your request available to you as soon as reasonably possible after it receives your deposit. 
  
 The South Carolina Family Privacy Protection Act, SC Code section 30-2-10 et seq., prohibits a person or entity from obtaining or using personal information from records obtained from University for purposes of commercial solicitation as defined in that Act. A person or entity that violates the Act is subject to criminal penalties, including possible fines and imprisonment. If you obtain records from the University, you are certifying that you will not obtain or use these records for commercial solicitation in violation of the South Carolina Family Privacy Protection Act. 
  
 
Your deposit check should be forwarded to the following address:

FOIA
University Communications
University of South Carolina
Osborne Administration Building, Suite 107
Columbia, SC 29208 
  
 Please make out your check to "University of South Carolina" and indicate your request number somewhere on or attached to the check. 
  
 
We will acknowledge receipt of your check via email. 
  
 Please contact us if you have any questions in connection with the foregoing.

FOIA Coordinator
803-576-7715
foia@sc.edu
University Communications
University of South Carolina

title: Alumni Status on Faculty and Staff

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Alumni Status on Faculty and Staff
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the purpose of this request, faculty and staff are defined as two separate populations.

Records showing:

1. Total number of Faculty at the Virginia Military Institute.
2. The structure, composition, and procedures of the Virginia Military Institute Faculty Senate or its equivalent.
3. Total number of the VMI Faculty who are alumni of the Virginia Military Institute, with breakdown by department.
4. Total number of  the Virginia Military Institute Staff
5. Total number of the Virginia Military Institute Staff by Department or Office who are VMI Alumni.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request #26-40
communications.communication:
The response to this request appears to be incomplete. The record released starts at Para #3, and all above it is redacted. 

1. Why was the entire document not released?

2. Portions of the record are redacted without explanation and justification.

3.  The structure of the Faculty Senate and some of its procedures (subject to the issue on redaction) are provided, but the response does not include the COMPOSITION of the Virginia Military Institute Faculty Senate or its equivalent in terms of WHO the members of the Faculty Senate are.

Under Virginia FOIA (§ 2.2-3704.01), public bodies must disclose non-exempt portions of records while redacting exempt material. They must provide a written response that cites the specific Virginia Code section authorizing the redaction and identifies the redacted subject matter with reasonable particularity.

Please provide the COMPOSITION of the Faculty Senate or its equivalent (who they are, departments, etc.) and respond with the required information on the redactions.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request #26-40
communications.communication:
In addition, after reviewing the spreadsheet, the information you provided on faculty and staff is incomplete and therefore not compliant with the FOIA.

1. I requested "Total number of the VMI Faculty who are alumni of the Virginia Military Institute, WITH BREAKOUT BY DEPARTMENT." 

- You did not provide the breakout by department. 

2. I requested "Total number of the Virginia Military Institute Staff BY DEPARTMENT OR OFFICE - Please clarify 

- Are the only offices at VMI that contain alumni: the Commandant's Office, the Superintendent's Office, and the Finance and Support Office?

3. You list 5 as "Academics" - is this that only five members of the VMI Faculty are VMI Alumni? If so, regarding my first reply on the issue of redactions and incomplete information, you did not identify which departments these three faculty members are in.

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Virginia Military Institute public records request #26-40
communications.communication:
Virginia Military Institute ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-40: * The entire document (Faculty Handbook) is available at the website link provided. The relevant section was provided for your convenience. * You're right. That was confusing. Per #1, the entire document is publicly available on the website link provided in our original response. * VMI does not have a Faculty Senate. The Faculty Advisory Council's membership is listed at the website link provided. * You were provided a record that already exists and that most closely meets your request. VMI is not obligated to create a new record (Va. Code § 2.2-3704.(D). * You were provided a record that shows alumni by operating unit. VMI is not obligated to create a new record (Va. Code § 2.2-3704.(D). * You were provided a record that already exists and most closely meets your request. VMI is not obligated to create a new record (Va. Code § 2.2-3704.(D). ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? title: PD Settlement Agreements

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: PD Settlement Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

 A copy of every agreement to settle a lawsuit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim against this agency over the past 10 years. This covers all agreements that would fall under New Hampshire RSA 91-A:4 (Right-to-Know Law), VI:

VI. Every agreement to settle a lawsuit against a governmental unit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim, entered into by any political subdivision or its insurer, shall be kept on file at the municipal clerk's office and made available for public inspection for a period of no less than 10 years from the date of settlement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Police Staff Roster (January 2024) (Bellingham Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police Staff Roster (January 2024) (Bellingham Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), OpenDocument spreadsheet, Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Safe Community

communications.subject: Police Department Records Request :: P031117-100625
communications.communication:
Dear Safe Community: Please note that while staff continue to provide the fullest assistance to all requestors, our response times may be delayed due to staffing shortages across the city as a result of the on-going COVID-19 pandemic. Your patience is very much appreciated. 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Belligham Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 10/6/2025 and given the reference number P031117-100625 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), OpenDocument spreadsheet, Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Safe Community

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2101520/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Bellingham Police Department

We understand your request for a "staff roster" to be referring to the staff roster of the Bellingham Police Department. If this understanding is incorrect, please let us know as soon as possible. 
I am writing to acknowledge receipt of your request and to provide a reasonable estimate of the time the City will need to respond to it. The City needs additional time to locate and assemble the requested records and to determine whether any of the requested records are exempt and that a denial should be made as to all or part of the request. Additionally, we consider current staffing levels and the number of requests in our public disclosure backlog when estimating response time. For these reasons, I estimate that the City will provide records to you on or before 10/31/25. 

Please note that the police department may also exercise its right to provide the responsive records in installments as they become available. 

You may contact Police Records at (360) 778-8700 if you have any questions about this request. 

Sincerely, 
Bellingham Police Records Division

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P031117-100625
communications.communication:
To Whom it May Concern,

Could you please provide an update on the status of this request? This is vital information that should be readily accessible to our community as the positions and salaries of each and every person employed in your department is funded by tax payer dollars. There are not pages and pages to sift through nor are there redactions needed for this request, and two months is an excessively long delay for a roster. If there is a hold up with the requested format, please let us know. We would accept the release in batches if there is an alternate format that could be provided immediately in the interim while the request is processed. 

Thank you for your time on this matter.

Sincerely,

Safe Community

We apologize for the delay and appreciate your patience. Due to employee vacations/sick time/other absences, a large backlog of records requests received before this request, and due to the fact that there is no one individual record that contains all of the information you have requested, we have been unable to complete this request until this week. A first installment is ready for you to review or download at the following link: Police Department Records Request - P031117-100625 
Be advised that, as there is no single record that contains all of the requested information, this record is a new document created as a courtesy to more efficiently fulfill your request. Also note that this record originally contained employee home address and phone number information that was redacted per RCW 42.56.250(1)(d). Normally we would provide this information in a redacted PDF, however, as you have requested not to receive a redacted PDF, we have chosen to omit that information from the excel export. 
We are searching for additional responsive records and anticipate having them to you, or providing an update on this request, on or before 12/19/25. 
If you have additional questions or concerns, please call Records Supervisor Revell at 360-778-8713. 
Sincerely, 
Bellingham Police Records Division

title: City of Watsonville: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded to Watsonville city website

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Watsonville: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded to Watsonville city website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded via the city of Watsonville's website. 

Time frame for request: June 1st 2023 to March 1st 2026. 

The photographs would have been uploaded using this form:

https://www.watsonville.gov/FormCenter/Public-Works-16/Report-Graffiti-106

This is a request for just the photographs. This is not a request for any other identifying information. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of Watsonville. 

Thank you for the city of Watsonville's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: Your City of Watsonville, CA public records request #26-116 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-116 has been submitted successfully. Dear Requester:  The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. Within the next ten (10) days, we will determine whether your request seeks copies of disclosable records in the City's possession or whether the City will require an extension. Sincerely,   City Clerk's Office (831) 768-3048 275 Main Street, Suite 400, Watsonville, CA 95076 FAX: 831-761-0736 E-mail:  cityclerk@watsonville.gov Open Monday - Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact City of Watsonville, CA.

Change your email settings | Visit our help center

communications.subject: Your City of Watsonville, CA public records request #26-116 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ Record request #26-116 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Robert Delaware, The City of Watsonville (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on March 1, 2026 (“Request”). The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act (“PRA”). (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for the record, that record is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the record is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions, requests for information, or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. The City has determined that it possesses non-exempt records responsive to your Request, and provides those records to you. With this letter and production, the City considers this Request complete. If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@watsonville.gov. Sincerely,   City Clerk's Office (831) 768-3010 275 Main Street, Suite 400, Watsonville, CA 95076 FAX: 831-761-0736 E-mail:  cityclerk@watsonville.gov Open Monday - Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Watsonville, CA.

Technical support: See our help page

title: Arizona Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety audits and network sharing (Maricopa County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Arizona Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety audits and network sharing (Maricopa County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:

1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of December 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of December 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Flock networks shared with me Devices List (Please see https://cdn.muckrock.com/foia_files/2022/11/17/CPRA_REQUEST.pdf
https://cdn.muckrock.com/foia_files/2022/11/09/1938_001.pdf as examples of the lists from other jurisdictions.
3c. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Paul Tepper

communications.subject: FOIA Recieved 2160
communications.communication:
Greetings,

The Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office has received your request for public records and is processing your request. All requests are processed in the order in which they are received, and no requests are prioritized by deadline date. The amount of time to completely process your request depends upon the number of pending requests, volume of material encompassed in the request, and the amount of redaction, if any, that is necessary.

Please be aware, if you are requesting records with large amount of body camera videos and or high-profile cases, the time to complete your request may be significantly longer as these cases are often voluminous. Identifying the specific record(s) being requested may help expedite your request. You will be notified once records are made available for you to download or pick up, or if no records are found. Your tracking # is 2160. When checking the status of your request, please send emails to mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov<mailto:mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov> and include your tracking #.

***Pursuant to ARS 39-129, the Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office (MCSO) Legal Liaison Section has implemented a fee schedule for requests for video recordings.  The fee for processing video recordings is $25 per each video-hour ($6.25 per quarter hour).  In the event there are contemporaneous multiple requests for the same video, the cost associated with processing the video will be shared equally between all requesting parties at that time. Requests for previously processed videos will be at no cost unless the requestor wishes to receive a hard copy instead of an electronic copy. Fees for hardcopies are listed below. ***

The fee breakdown for hard copies are as follows:

Documents:                       $.50 for each page

CD’s:                                      $15.00 each

Thank you

—
Calbert Gillett
[Sent from Front]

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Louis County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Louis County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe
