title: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: City of Benicia, CA. - Public Records Act Request Records Request PR-2026-53
communications.communication:
RE: Record Request Request Number: PR-2026-53 Submitted on: 02/25/2026 
  
 Dear , 
  
 This letter shall serve as the City’s formal written response to your request under the California Public Records Act (Government Code section 7920 et seq.). 
  
 Under established California Law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs or reports in response to a request. 
  
 This communication shall serve as the City’s additional formal written response. 
  
 Upon review of your request, the City is still in the process of determining whether the request in whole or in part seeks copies of disclosable public records in possession of the City. Pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535(c), the 
  
 City may extend the time to respond for the following reasons: 
  
 The need to search for an collect the requested records from field facilities or other establishments that are separate from the office processing the request. 
  
 me to research your request for the reason noted above. Accordingly, the City will respond again to your request on or before March 23, 2026. 
  
 Thank you, 
  
 Ashley Bussart, Deputy City Clerk
abussart@ci.benicia.ca.us
707.746.4200

communications.subject: City of Benicia, CA. - Police Records Request Records Request PR-2026-53 FINAL -  Responsive Records - Case Summary
communications.communication:
RE: Record Request Request Number: PR-2026-53 Submitted on: 02/25/2026 
  
 Dear , 
  
 This letter shall serve as the City’s formal written response to your request under the California Public Records Act (Government Code section 7920 et seq.). 
  
 Under established California Law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs or reports in response to a request. 
  
 The City is required to determine whether the request, in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records. Consistent with that requirement, the City has conducted a search, and responsive records were found. 
  
 Please be aware that the California Public Records Act section 7923.600 et seq. exempts records of investigation from disclosure. (Haynie v. Superior Court (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1061.) However, summary information derived from those records may be required to be disclosed, as such the City is providing the following summary information for responsive events. 
  
 Benicia Police case number 26-00170 : 
  
 (A) The time, date, and location of occurrence. – 02/03/26 @ 0627 hrs, 5800 Blk of Cabernet Dr, Vallejo, CA 
  
 (B) The time and date of the report. – 02/03/26 @ 1229 hrs 
  
 (C) The name and age of the victim. – n/a 
  
 (D) The factual circumstances surrounding the crime or incident. – Agency assist for execution of a residential search warrant. 
  
 (E) A general description of any injuries, property, or weapons involved. – n/a 
  
 The City considers this request fulfilled. If you require any additional information or have any further questions you may contact Solano County Sheriff Department. 
  
 Thank you, 
  
 Suzie Kaluza, Police Records Supervisor
skaluza@ci.benicia.ca.us
707.746.4245

title: JOAQUIN GUZMAN'S ESCAPE

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act.  I hereby request the following records:

I REQUEST ANY AND ALL CABLES AND COMMUNICATIONS SENT BY THE US EMBASSY IN MEXICO TO THE DEPARTMENT OF STATE ON JOAQUIN GUZMÁN'S ESCAPE FROM A MEXICAN MAXIMUM SECURITY PRISON ON JULY 11 TH, 2015. PLEASE INCLUDE ANY AND ALL COMUNICATIONS SENT BETWEEN JULY 11TH AND SEPTEMBER 13TH REGARDING THIS MATTER.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Dyer
An acknowledgement letter, stating the request is being processed.
A letter stating the request for reduced or waived fees has been accepted.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Request #F-2015-15554
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Dyer,
This is in response to your faxed letter transmitted on December 13, 2017 regarding FOIA request case control number F-2015-15554. The Office of Information Programs and Services' (IPS) electronic records system indicates that your request has been assigned to the appropriate team for processing. We will provide an estimated date of completion to you when it becomes available.
The Department takes its FOIA responsibilities seriously. We currently have a backlog of approximately 11,200 cases. As a result, there could be a delay in processing your request. For your information, the Department of State has a collection of records on its website and makes monthly postings of records previously released in response to FOIA requests. You may search the Department's website at www.foia.state.gov<http://www.foia.state.gov> http://www.foia.state.gov for records that may be responsive to your request.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Request #F-2015-15554
communications.communication:
Mr. Dyer,



This is in response to your email below regarding the status of FOIA Case Control Number F-2015-15554.



The Office of Information Programs and Services' electronic records system indicates that your request remains in process.  You will be notified of the Department's search and review efforts in response to this request as soon as it becomes available.



The Department currently has a backlog of approximately 10,800 cases.  It is taking significant steps to improve its responsiveness to the public and has undertaken a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) surge project to reduce the backlog in the coming months.  In order to further the core FOIA goals of transparency and accountability, the State Department is committing more resources and personnel to clear the backlog of requests, while still maintaining the highest standards of quality.



As a result, there is presently a delay in the completion of FOIA and Privacy Act requests.


If you have any questions, please contact the FOIA Requester Service Center (FRSC) at (202) 261-8484<tel:(202)%20261-8484>, or fax us at (202) 261-8579<tel:(202)%20261-8579> or send an e-mail to foiastatus@state.gov<mailto:foiastatus@state.gov>.
Regards,

U.S. Department of State
FOIA Requester Service Center

On Oct. 14, 2015:
Subject:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

I REQUEST ANY AND ALL CABLES AND COMMUNICATIONS SENT BY THE US EMBASSY IN MEXICO TO THE DEPARTMENT OF STATE ON JOAQUIN GUZMÁN'S ESCAPE FROM A MEXICAN MAXIMUM SECURITY PRISON ON JULY 11 TH, 2015. PLEASE INCLUDE ANY AND ALL COMUNICATIONS SENT BETWEEN JULY 11TH AND SEPTEMBER 13TH REGARDING THIS MATTER.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Dyer

title: 2026 Brady List (Pierce County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Pierce County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PCPAO Reference No. 26-0010
communications.communication:
Hi Adrian,

Thank you for your quick response to my request!

In my request, I also asked for "accompanying impeachment information", which may be the material linked in the "LINK TO DISCOVERY" column. Would you please provide that as well?

Thank you,
Rose

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PCPAO Reference No. 26-0010
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I had interpreted your request as only requesting current Brady or PIE lists. Thank you for that clarification. I will reopen your request to search for the “accompanying impeachment information” for the individuals on the lists that were produced to you on January 12.

At this time, we anticipate that the next installment of records will be available to you on or before Thursday, February 19, 2026. If we determine that additional time is needed to complete your request, you will be notified. Should you have questions or concerns regarding this request, please contact our office referencing PCPAO Reference No. 26-0010.

Thank you,
Adrian McDaniel
Public Records Officer
Prosecuting Attorney | Civil Division

RE: PIERCE COUNTY Prosecuting Attorney PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST, Reference # P041339-010526. 
 
Dear Rose Terse, 
A production of records responsive to your request is ready for release. These records meet the fee threshold, as outlined in Pierce County Code 2.04.030(E). Please see the attached invoice, INV26-P041339-2. 
 
To make a payment online, log back into your Pierce County Public Records Request portal account and select "View My Invoices". Select "Make a Payment" for this invoice and follow the prompts to enter your payment information. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXXndrKlkWAcXKs3RKk0x6-2B8pdCk0ipTOgEFrezx8dJEGTEGSWYfnvF8UlMzIRC67G-2BfU3pkPbhhu86G19FienkUzJeTLf3Wh6ipnaLWflqnq2Q2vSDVkavEiXYzyby6S43YxpWOfFDYeLU-2BC8CZGwVWRuvZ-2FJbCEjlDiyWLk-2F9VvHCgNWPTwfTwYy9oubLU52g-3D-3DjQw6_cZC2DE3u2cs2QgRIye4LE-2FUuF21C7Nw2WTsI40J0tkEB7tIy1FyNVop12O496qbghtbzawlLBdX57vx3EgqRGDjoqk-2BCuTKjCptvxqCnHHbl25SlzzhVusYnUayysjbEDX2b20VFJZEIFdnD8CgW1uIJOO6HpCHdpAIpoEp0T6WVqZZwA3OcooD-2BHBQN6DC7vPI5vAGNBGa1O25kkMxxvfalOJn6EdUDoAdvF1RCumSi1r7VjeIVYaAt6GpSZqTqg-2BJcktUQskyhylt9n1EJYcxJAy77Rc-2Bi5ek0unwcVuRT5w9gnWnHYaT5EXhgVbc4MPnDvEBY3g4cPXpzDJ-2BSyFxa-2FGE7M-2BYx0R0bx4HpT-2FPjKmcnXeSIyThvYgxpkI9-2B) 
Alternatively, you may remit your payment to the Pierce County address on your invoice. Please include a copy of the invoice or note the Reference Number of the request with your payment so that we may apply your payment to the appropriate request. If no reference number is noted, we may not be able to determine which request to apply the payment. For cash payments, exact change is required. Personal checks will not be accepted as a method of payment. Cashier checks or money orders must be made payable to Pierce County. Processing of payment may take up to one business day, regardless of method of payment. 
Responsive records will be released to you upon completion of processing of payment. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(4) and Pierce County Code 2.04.040(J) if you do not pay the deposit or any installment payment due for the requested copies within 30 calendar days after receiving this notice, by the due date on the invoice, this agency may stop providing further installments of records and close the request. 
 
Respectfully, 
Prosecuting Attorney

title: Incident Report Metadata Request (Spokane Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Incident Report Metadata Request (Spokane Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For purposes of this request, “officer” means a sworn member of the Division of Police of any rank, and “data” means logs, tables, or datasets maintained in a machine-readable format (such as spreadsheets or database exports).

Datasets maintained by or on behalf of city relating to police incident reports metadata that includes the data points listed below.

The timeframe for all portions of this request is all responsive records that exist from January 1, 2014 to the most recent date that data is collected.

For each reported crime incident, please provide the following data fields, to the extent they exist in a machine-readable format:

Unique identifier that would allow matching between other datasets, for example, matching to 911 CAD data
Case Number or Incident Number
Report Number (if separate)
Incident Date and Time
Report Date and Time
Offense Date and Time (start and end, if tracked)
Offense Type / Crime Type
Offense Description
Statute or Code Section
Statute Description
Offense Classification (e.g., felony, misdemeanor, degree, UCR/NIBRS category)
Incident Location (address or block-level location)
City Council District, Precinct, Zone, or equivalent geographic designation
Clearance Status (e.g., open, cleared by arrest, exceptional clearance)
Clearance Date (if applicable)
Number of Offenses Associated with the Incident
Domestic Violence Indicator (if tracked)
Hate Crime Indicator (if tracked)
Weapon Indicator or Weapon Type (if tracked)

Responding Officer Information (if maintained at the incident level)
Primary Responding Officer Badge Number
Primary Responding Officer Rank or Title
Primary Responding Officer Unit or Assignment at the time of the incident

Optional, if maintained in the system
Victim Demographic Information (aggregated or anonymized, if applicable)
Suspect Demographic Information (aggregated or anonymized, if applicable)

Similar to the aforementioned public records request, please provide a codebook or data dictionary so that the data provided can be appropriately interpreted. If a Zoom call or meeting is preferred in lieu of a written codebook, that is acceptable.

If you deny any portion of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available under the law.

The requested records will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If there are fees associated with fulfilling this request, please inform me of the total charges in advance. I prefer that records be provided electronically, by email attachment if available, or by CD-ROM if necessary.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation. I look forward to receiving your response within the time period required by law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Filed Requests

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2174941/ RCW 42.56.520 requires a Washington state agency to respond to a public records request within five business days.  That response will be one of the following: provide the records, or; provide a reasonable estimate of time to fulfill the request, or; seek clarification on the request, or; deny the request with a written explanation. The five-day clock starts the first business day after the request is received, excluding Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. Public records fees may apply.  Find out more about public record fees here: https://my.spokanecity.org/administrative/public-records/. Your request will be forwarded to the relevant City department(s) to locate the records you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request.  You will be contacted about the availability of the records in question.

Dear Filed Requests 
This letter will serve as acknowledgement we are in receipt of the above identified request for records. Be advised additional time will be needed: 
to locate and assemble the information requested, 
to notify any third persons or agencies affected by the request; and 
to determine whether any of the information requested is exempt and a denial should be made as to all or part of the request. 
Due to current staffing and volume of requests received by Spokane Police Division, we estimate forwarding you the first installment of responsive records, or a response as to why these records are exempt from inspection per state statutes, in approximately 63 days or March 31st, 2026. 

If we are able to provide you with the records or a response sooner than the anticipated date, we will do so without delay. 

1544 
Public Records Specialist 
Spokane Police Department

Spokane Police Department File(s)will be available to view/download in GovQA for 60 days, at which time they will be deleted due to storage limits in the system.  You may choose to download files to your personal storage device for permanent retention if desired. Go to "My Request Center" and find the request. Disable your browser's pop-up blockers to be able to download files. 
a. If you are encountering difficulty retrieving your responsive records from an email, from your portal, or from the public archive on your Apple device(s), you may need to disable your pop-up blocker on your device. 
b. Apple mobile devices default to Pop-up blocker ON. 
c. To change your settings on your device:

  * Safari-> 
  * Block Pop-ups->

This act establishes a strong state mandate in favor of disclosure of public records.  As such, the information you submit to the City via email, including personal information, may ultimately be subject to disclosure as a public record. Review of denials of public records Any person who objects to the initial denial or partial denial of a record request may petition in writing (including e-mail) to the public records officer for a review of that decision. The petition shall include a copy of or reasonably identify the written statement by the public records officer or designee denying the request. The public records officer shall promptly provide the petition and any other relevant information to the City Hearing Examiner, with a copy to the City Attorney for City Police records and to the Chief Deputy Attorney for County Sheriff records. The City Attorney or designee or Chief Deputy Attorney for County Sheriff or designee may file a response to the petition with the Hearing Examiner. Any response to the petition must be filed with the Hearing Examiner within five (5) business days following the City’s receipt of the appeal. The Hearing Examiner will consider the petition and either affirm, modify, or reverse the denial within ten (10) business days following the Spokane Police Department’s receipt of the petition, or within such greater period of time as the Spokane Police Department and the requestor mutually agree to.

03/10/2026 
 
RE: PUBLIC RECORD REQUEST #P000538-011626 for "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

For purposes of this request, “officer” means a sworn member of the Division of Police of any rank, and “data” means logs, tables, or datasets maintained in a machine-readable format (such as spreadsheets or database exports). 

Datasets maintained by or on behalf of city relating to police incident reports metadata that includes the data points listed below. 

The timeframe for all portions of this request is all responsive records that exist from January 1, 2014 to the most recent date that data is collected. 

For each reported crime incident, please provide the following data fields, to the extent they exist in a machine-readable format: 

Unique identifier that would allow matching between other datasets, for example, matching to 911 CAD data 
Case Number or Incident Number 
Report Number (if separate) 
Incident Date and Time 
Report Date and Time 
Offense Date and Time (start and end, if tracked) 
Offense Type / Crime Type 
Offense Description 
Statute or Code Section 
Statute Description 
Offense Classification (e.g., felony, misdemeanor, degree, UCR/NIBRS category) 
Incident Location (address or block-level location) 
City Council District, Precinct, Zone, or equivalent geographic designation 
Clearance Status (e.g., open, cleared by arrest, exceptional clearance) 
Clearance Date (if applicable) 
Number of Offenses Associated with the Incident 
Domestic Violence Indicator (if tracked) 
Hate Crime Indicator (if tracked) 
Weapon Indicator or Weapon Type (if tracked) 

Responding Officer Information (if maintained at the incident level) 
Primary Responding Officer Badge Number 
Primary Responding Officer Rank or Title 
Primary Responding Officer Unit or Assignment at the time of the incident 

Optional, if maintained in the system 
Victim Demographic Information (aggregated or anonymized, if applicable) 
Suspect Demographic Information (aggregated or anonymized, if applicable) 

Similar to the aforementioned public records request, please provide a codebook or data dictionary so that the data provided can be appropriately interpreted. If a Zoom call or meeting is preferred in lieu of a written codebook, that is acceptable. 

If you deny any portion of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available under the law. 

The requested records will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: FLOCK - Greater Cincinnati (Forest Park Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety automated license plate readers over the last 30 days
- The names of the hot lists/watch lists used with Flock Safety automated license plate readers 
- The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- All agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
- All contracts, MOUs, or other agreements with Flock Safety
- All procurement documents related to Flock Safety

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Evan Cooper

title: Richmond Monuments - City

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Richmond Monuments - City
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, Va. Code § 2.2-3700 et seq., I request access to and copies of all records created or received between June 1, 2021 and the present concerning the removal, disposition, donation, transfer, or ultimate use of the City of Richmond’s Confederate monuments/statues, including the Lee statue and pedestal.

Please include:

Evaluation & Scoring Records – All versions/drafts of the Monuments Criteria Evaluation Score Sheet (Mar. 22, 2021), the Monuments Requests Research & Review Matrix (Sept. 9, 2021), reviewer notes, deliberation materials, recommendation memos, meeting agendas/minutes, and recordings.

Council Actions – Drafts, staff reports, attachments, notices, hearing records, and communications relating to Resolution 2022-R002 (Jan. 24, 2022) and ORD. 2022-001.

Disposition & Transfer Records – Donation agreements, bills of sale, custody/transport records, storage records, MOUs or collaboration agreements with the Black History Museum & Cultural Center of Virginia (BHMVA) and The Valentine.

Executive Coordination – Records concerning the joint Dec. 30, 2021 plan/announcement with the Governor’s Office regarding donation to BHMVA and collaboration with The Valentine.

Applications & Endorsements – All submissions and endorsements received by the City during the monuments disposition process, including emails or correspondence forwarding endorsements.

Communications – All emails, texts, chats, and attachments between City officials and the following individuals/entities:

The Valentine: Bill Martin, Christina Vida

BHMVA: Andrea Douglas, Marland Buckner, Trinity Swift

Jefferson School African American Heritage Center/JSAAHC: Swords Into Plowshares (SIP)

UVA: Melody Barnes, Claire Antone Payton, Jalane Schmidt, Laurent Dubois, Frank Dukes, Ellen Laughlin Ochs

Others: Renée Grisham, Tom Perriello, Sally Hudson, Jennifer McClellan

Search terms: “Swords Into Plowshares,” SIP, “Jefferson School,” JSAAHC, “Valentine Museum,” “Black History Museum,” BHMVA, “Lee statue,” melt, destroy, recast, endorsement, coalition.

Political Figures – All communications with (a) members of the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus (VLBC), (b) Virginia State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, and (c) Rep. Abigail Spanberger. See Appendix for full names list.

Please provide records in native electronic format where available. If any portion is withheld, cite the specific FOIA exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Richmond Monuments - City
communications.communication:
Tom Nash – We are happy to assist and provide an estimated advance cost determination for the requested undertaking, as provided for under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, which would include making reasonable charges, not to exceed the actual cost incurred in accessing, duplicating, supplying, and searching for any responsive record/document. This provision includes, but is not limited to, Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37, Sections 2.2-3700 through 3714. (et seq. and etc.), Section 2.2-3704; Parts F and H, as follows:

With regard to the requests, please find the attached information and linked documents regarding the Monuments (provided on the Richmond City Council Website, at https://www.rva.gov/richmond-city-council/confederate-monuments-disposition) which may provide some documents you are seeking:

In conservatively estimating the undertaking for required work involved in searching for requested records, pending clarifications cited below that would need to be clarified for reasonable specificity to proceed, an advance conservative cost determination estimate is $2,815, which, due to pending clarifications and the potential amount of work required, the final cost could likely be greater, possibly lesser.

As a next step, please clarify the names and contact emails/information for:


  1.  “(a) members of the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus (VLBC)” during the requested timeframe and
  2.  “(b) Virginia State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, and”
  3.  (c) Rep. Abigail Spanberger”
  4.  Please provide “See Appendix for full names list.” (NOTE: no appendix provided)


In hopes of being as helpful and cost efficient as possible on your behalf, please provide any/all specificity, clarifications, refinements, and updates to your request that you can in helping identifying types, names, etc. of such record/s that could assist in faithfully understanding and comprehending intended size, scope, and variables for what might all be involved and included that would assist in being as comprehensive for searching and as conservative as possibly for assessing, staffing, planning, and processing the searching on your behalf and possibly lessening time and cost required.

If you wish to clarify and update your requests and for us to continue to process and proceed with searching, as provided for by the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, please send an advance deposit in the amount above, which would be credited to the final cost, payable to the City of Richmond (Re: Richmond City Council VFOIA records Search Request), to Steve Skinner, RE: VFOIA Records request, Richmond City Council Office of Council Chief of Staff, Richmond City Hall, 900 E. Broad Street, Suite 300, Richmond, Virginia 23219.

The deposit will be credited toward the final cost (which could likely be greater, possibly lesser) of supplying any responsive records if they exist. The statutory period for response will be tolled and the request will not continue to be processed until the deposit in the amount of the estimate provided above has been received.



·       Due to the potential scope, variables, and coordinated effort involved and required for undertaking, pending any additional clarifications you provide and actual work required for accessing, searching, duplicating, and supplying any responsive requested records, would and may include working with multiple staffs, staff members, systems, accounts, the Richmond Department of Information Technology, etc. if/as needed, creating, producing, and providing databases and/or files for accessing, searching, duplicating, and supplying any records that exist, which would involve and require significant effort, scheduling, process, etc.  and require practicable time to accomplish.



·       This would also include all required due diligence provided for by the Virginia Freedom of Information Act and any required/needed review and/or legal assistance of the Richmond Office of the City Attorney (for which costs of which cannot be determined at this time).



·       Regarding such above, it is not practically possible to provide records that are responsive to your request, if they exist, or determine if such are available within a five-work-day period and an additional seven workdays would be required and invoked, as per § 2.2-3704 subsection B 4 of to provide an additional seven working days to respond to your request.



·       It also is likely that, due to the size of the undertaking, such would require additional practicable time to accomplish, per variables, specifications, scheduling, staffing, etc., which we would be happy to discuss moving forward.

Notes on your behalf: Per Virginia Freedom of Information Act provisions assistance, estimates, costs, specifications, and clarifications regarding size, scope, extent, Parameters, variables, etc.:


·       We are happy to assist in searching for specified records and an estimated advance cost determinations for requested work/undertaking, as provided for under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, can be provided for searching for records in advance.



·       A good faith advance approximation determination of anticipated cost estimate would include making reasonable charges, not to exceed the actual cost incurred in accessing, duplicating, supplying, and searching for any responsive records in advance to proceed, if requested. Please let us know if you would like to proceed receiving cost estimate. Under the Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37, Virginia Freedom of Information Act; Section 2.2-3700, et seq. etc.


·       An advance cost estimate includes costs required in estimating undertaking as to the extent and amount of work that would be required and identifying the most expedient and least expensive way to achieve on behalf of requester.



·       It is important to note that advance estimates are good faith approximations and not final/exact costs, as such would not be known until the final work was completed, which, depending on the scope, variables, and possible coordinated efforts involved and due to the potential amount of all the work required, the final actual costs could likely be greater, possibly lesser.



·       The Virginia Freedom of Information Act includes, but is not limited to, Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37, Virginia Freedom of Information Act; Sections 2.2-3700 through 3714. (et seq. and etc.), which includes Section 2.2-3704; Parts F and H, as follows:



“… H. In any case where a public body determines in advance that charges for producing the requested records are likely to exceed $200, the public body may, before continuing to process the request, require the requester to pay a deposit not to exceed the amount of the advance determination...”



“F… Any costs incurred by the public body in estimating the cost of supplying the requested records shall be applied toward the overall charges to be paid by the requester for the supplying of such requested records.”.


·       Per the Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37., “Day One" of a request is considered the next business day after the business day your request is received. If a request was submitted after Richmond govt. has closed for the day, or on a city holiday, or if the Richmond govt./the city is for some reason closed, or if on a weekend, the request will be considered received on the next business day after it was received. A public body has five working days to respond, which does not count City holidays (such as the recent within this timeframe City government closure for the Juneteenth Holiday on June 19, 2025), if the Richmond govt./City is for some reason closed, or weekend days (ie: Saturday and Sunday).



Request Sent                Tuesday, August 19, 2025 3:55 PM

On Aug. 29, 2025:
Subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Richmond Monuments - City
Thank you for the information and clarifications. I will review this in detail and respond. Given this information I may be able to further scope the request to save cost but especially workload on your part.
---

On Aug. 26, 2025:
Subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Richmond Monuments - City
Tom Nash – We are happy to assist and provide an estimated advance cost determination for the requested undertaking, as provided for under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, which would include making reasonable charges, not to exceed the actual cost incurred in accessing, duplicating, supplying, and searching for any responsive record/document. This provision includes, but is not limited to, Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37, Sections 2.2-3700 through 3714. (et seq. and etc.), Section 2.2-3704; Parts F and H, as follows:

With regard to the requests, please find the attached information and linked documents regarding the Monuments (provided on the Richmond City Council Website, at https://www.rva.gov/richmond-city-council/confederate-monuments-disposition) which may provide some documents you are seeking:

In conservatively estimating the undertaking for required work involved in searching for requested records, pending clarifications cited below that would need to be clarified for reasonable specificity to proceed, an advance conservative cost determination estimate is $2,815, which, due to pending clarifications and the potential amount of work required, the final cost could likely be greater, possibly lesser.

As a next step, please clarify the names and contact emails/information for:

1. “(a) members of the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus (VLBC)” during the requested timeframe and
2. “(b) Virginia State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, and”
3. (c) Rep. Abigail Spanberger”
4. Please provide “See Appendix for full names list.” (NOTE: no appendix provided)

In hopes of being as helpful and cost efficient as possible on your behalf, please provide any/all specificity, clarifications, refinements, and updates to your request that you can in helping identifying types, names, etc. of such record/s that could assist in faithfully understanding and comprehending intended size, scope, and variables for what might all be involved and included that would assist in being as comprehensive for searching and as conservative as possibly for assessing, staffing, planning, and processing the searching on your behalf and possibly lessening time and cost required.

If you wish to clarify and update your requests and for us to continue to process and proceed with searching, as provided for by the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, please send an advance deposit in the amount above, which would be credited to the final cost, payable to the City of Richmond (Re: Richmond City Council VFOIA records Search Request), to Steve Skinner, RE: VFOIA Records request, Richmond City Council Office of Council Chief of Staff, Richmond City Hall, 900 E. Broad Street, Suite 300, Richmond, Virginia 23219.

The deposit will be credited toward the final cost (which could likely be greater, possibly lesser) of supplying any responsive records if they exist. The statutory period for response will be tolled and the request will not continue to be processed until the deposit in the amount of the estimate provided above has been received.

· Due to the potential scope, variables, and coordinated effort involved and required for undertaking, pending any additional clarifications you provide and actual work required for accessing, searching, duplicating, and supplying any responsive requested records, would and may include working with multiple staffs, staff members, systems, accounts, the Richmond Department of Information Technology, etc. if/as needed, creating, producing, and providing databases and/or files for accessing, searching, duplicating, and supplying any records that exist, which would involve and require significant effort, scheduling, process, etc. and require practicable time to accomplish.

· This would also include all required due diligence provided for by the Virginia Freedom of Information Act and any required/needed review and/or legal assistance of the Richmond Office of the City Attorney (for which costs of which cannot be determined at this time).

· Regarding such above, it is not practically possible to provide records that are responsive to your request, if they exist, or determine if such are available within a five-work-day period and an additional seven workdays would be required and invoked, as per § 2.2-3704 subsection B 4 of to provide an additional seven working days to respond to your request.

· It also is likely that, due to the size of the undertaking, such would require additional practicable time to accomplish, per variables, specifications, scheduling, staffing, etc., which we would be happy to discuss moving forward.

Notes on your behalf: Per Virginia Freedom of Information Act provisions assistance, estimates, costs, specifications, and clarifications regarding size, scope, extent, Parameters, variables, etc.:

· We are happy to assist in searching for specified records and an estimated advance cost determinations for requested work/undertaking, as provided for under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, can be provided for searching for records in advance.

· A good faith advance approximation determination of anticipated cost estimate would include making reasonable charges, not to exceed the actual cost incurred in accessing, duplicating, supplying, and searching for any responsive records in advance to proceed, if requested. Please let us know if you would like to proceed receiving cost estimate. Under the Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37, Virginia Freedom of Information Act; Section 2.2-3700, et seq. etc.

· An advance cost estimate includes costs required in estimating undertaking as to the extent and amount of work that would be required and identifying the most expedient and least expensive way to achieve on behalf of requester.

· It is important to note that advance estimates are good faith approximations and not final/exact costs, as such would not be known until the final work was completed, which, depending on the scope, variables, and possible coordinated efforts involved and due to the potential amount of all the work required, the final actual costs could likely be greater, possibly lesser.

· The Virginia Freedom of Information Act includes, but is not limited to, Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37, Virginia Freedom of Information Act; Sections 2.2-3700 through 3714. (et seq. and etc.), which includes Section 2.2-3704; Parts F and H, as follows:

“… H. In any case where a public body determines in advance that charges for producing the requested records are likely to exceed $200, the public body may, before continuing to process the request, require the requester to pay a deposit not to exceed the amount of the advance determination...”

“F… Any costs incurred by the public body in estimating the cost of supplying the requested records shall be applied toward the overall charges to be paid by the requester for the supplying of such requested records.”.

· Per the Virginia Code of Laws (Code of Virginia) Title 2.2, Chapter 37., “Day One" of a request is considered the next business day after the business day your request is received. If a request was submitted after Richmond govt. has closed for the day, or on a city holiday, or if the Richmond govt./the city is for some reason closed, or if on a weekend, the request will be considered received on the next business day after it was received. A public body has five working days to respond, which does not count City holidays (such as the recent within this timeframe City government closure for the Juneteenth Holiday on June 19, 2025), if the Richmond govt./City is for some reason closed, or weekend days (ie: Saturday and Sunday).

Request Received Tuesday, August 19, 2025 3:55 PM

First Day Wednesday, August 20, 2025

Fifth Day/DUE BY: Tuesday, August 26, 2025

Response Sent: Tuesday, August 26, 2025

Pease let us know if you have any questions, clarifications per above, and/or if/how we can be of assistance. :)

Steven R. Skinner
Richmond City Council
Virginia Freedom of Information Act Officer
RICHMOND CITY COUNCIL
Office of Council Chief of Staff

Steven R. Skinner

On Aug. 19, 2025:
Subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Richmond Monuments - City
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, Va. Code § 2.2-3700 et seq., I request access to and copies of all records created or received between June 1, 2021 and the present concerning the removal, disposition, donation, transfer, or ultimate use of the City of Richmond’s Confederate monuments/statues, including the Lee statue and pedestal.

Please include:

Evaluation & Scoring Records – All versions/drafts of the Monuments Criteria Evaluation Score Sheet (Mar. 22, 2021), the Monuments Requests Research & Review Matrix (Sept. 9, 2021), reviewer notes, deliberation materials, recommendation memos, meeting agendas/minutes, and recordings.

Council Actions – Drafts, staff reports, attachments, notices, hearing records, and communications relating to Resolution 2022-R002 (Jan. 24, 2022) and ORD. 2022-001.

Disposition & Transfer Records – Donation agreements, bills of sale, custody/transport records, storage records, MOUs or collaboration agreements with the Black History Museum & Cultural Center of Virginia (BHMVA) and The Valentine.

Executive Coordination – Records concerning the joint Dec. 30, 2021 plan/announcement with the Governor’s Office regarding donation to BHMVA and collaboration with The Valentine.

Applications & Endorsements – All submissions and endorsements received by the City during the monuments disposition process, including emails or correspondence forwarding endorsements.

Communications – All emails, texts, chats, and attachments between City officials and the following individuals/entities:

The Valentine: Bill Martin, Christina Vida

BHMVA: Andrea Douglas, Marland Buckner, Trinity Swift

Jefferson School African American Heritage Center/JSAAHC: Swords Into Plowshares (SIP)

UVA: Melody Barnes, Claire Antone Payton, Jalane Schmidt, Laurent Dubois, Frank Dukes, Ellen Laughlin Ochs

Others: Renée Grisham, Tom Perriello, Sally Hudson, Jennifer McClellan

Search terms: “Swords Into Plowshares,” SIP, “Jefferson School,” JSAAHC, “Valentine Museum,” “Black History Museum,” BHMVA, “Lee statue,” melt, destroy, recast, endorsement, coalition.

Political Figures – All communications with (a) members of the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus (VLBC), (b) Virginia State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, and (c) Rep. Abigail Spanberger. See Appendix for full names list.

Please provide records in native electronic format where available. If any portion is withheld, cite the specific FOIA exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (El Dorado County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (El Dorado County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

Dear Rose Terse: 
The first installment of records responsive to your public disclosure request is now available for download via your account on our Public Records Portal. 
If you have not logged in to your Public Records Portal to review or download these records by February 6, 2026, then we will consider your request to be abandoned, and will close it accordingly. communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
February 09, 2026 RE: W050882-010426 Dear Rose Terse:

"On January 8, 2026, we contacted you to let you know that records responsive to your request were available for download via our public records portal. To date, you have not downloaded these records. We therefore consider your request to be abandoned and closed. We will no longer be monitoring this request for any subsequent downloads.

If you have questions or concerns regarding your request, please feel free to contact me by phone at 360.753.8096, by responding to this email, or through our public records portal. We ask, however, that you please submit these to us within 30 days of this letter. Additionally, please note that contacting us about your request will not result in it being re-opened unless we tell you in writing that we have re-opened it to provide further responsive records.

This is the final response to your records request and the City of Olympia will not be responding further to your request with any additional responsive records. Should you wish to take any legal action to challenge the City’s response to your request, any such action must be filed within one year of the date of this letter.  You may also request an administrative review of your request if you consider that you have been denied records to this email or through our public records portal.

Sincerely,

Amy Iverson
Olympia Police Department"

Please note that some information has been redacted from the responsive documents pursuant to the Public Records Act. At the end of each document, you will find an exemption log which will explaining the redactions and cite the statutory basis for the redactions. If you believe that any of these exemptions have been claimed in error, please contact us immediately. If you have questions or concerns regarding your request, please feel free to contact me by phone at 360.753.8096, by responding to this email, or through our public records portal. We ask, however, that you please submit these to us within 30 days of this letter. Taylor Barnes

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Oneida County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Oneida County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: FW: EXTERNAL: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Jackson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon-

Jackson County is in receipt of your public records request below received on 1/28/26.  The County is the custodian of records.  We have begun working on reviewing our records to determine an estimate for your request.

In an initial review of the responsive records, it has been determined that we do have a spreadsheet of data, similar to what you requested.  However, it is likely not a complete set of data as the agency was not fully operational in the software throughout the time period you are looking for.  It does contain the majority of the data categories you are looking for, but not all of them.  The cost to provide this set of information would be significantly less than also pulling all individual reports.  For comparison, but not a hard estimate, we can likely provide the spreadsheet data in the hundreds of dollars range, compared to the thousands of dollars range for tracking down every individual report responsive to the request.  Again, this is general and only after a cursory review.
Would you be interested in an estimate for the spreadsheet data available or would you like us to continue to provide a full estimate of all data requested for the 9 year time period specified?

Please let me know so we can proceed further.

Sincerely,

Katie

Katie Zerkel
Senior Assistant County Counsel
Jackson County Counsel Office
10 S. Oakdale Ave. Room 214
Medford, OR 97501
Office: 541-774-6162 / Fax: 541-774-6722
Zerkelkm@jacksoncountyor.gov<mailto:Zerkelkm@jacksoncountyor.gov>

title: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records maintained by Alma Fuerte Public School for the period January 1, 2023 through the present relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on school property, including work performed with or without a formal contract, and regardless of dollar amount.

Specifically, I am requesting:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

This request seeks copies of existing records only and does not require the creation of new records.
If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please identify the specific exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Bennett

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request all material related to meetings of any kind held about or mentioning Flock, Flock Safety, or Flock Group as a significant topic. title: INCIDENT DOCUMENTATION Title: Incident Documentation - Wallingford Public Schools

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: INCIDENT DOCUMENTATION Title: Incident Documentation - Wallingford Public Schools
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All incident reports, security logs, or communications from April 1, 2024 to present involving John Conrad, Emelia Conrad, Sage Conrad, or the Conrad family
- Documentation of school access policies and their implementation, including visitor protocols
- Any communications with law enforcement agencies regarding school security matters involving the Conrad family from January 1, 2023 to present
- Records of any safety concerns, threat assessments, or protective measures discussed regarding school personnel or visitors from January 1, 2024 to present

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

john Conrad

title: Request to Air Force for list of 2021 film collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Air Force for list of 2021 film collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films that the US Air Force has participated in the production of, in any capacity whatsoever, for the year 2021. Any and all such lists, full or partial, would be appreciated and may include, but are not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Air Force for list of 2021 film collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films that the US Air Force has participated in the production of, in any capacity whatsoever, for the year 2021. Any and all such lists, full or partial, would be appreciated and may include, but are not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Request Acknowledgement - AF 2026-01591-F
communications.communication:
Dear Brett  Carollo, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your December 8, 2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for "A list of all the films that the US Air Force has participated in the production of, in any capacity whatsoever, for the year 2021. Any and all such lists, full or partial, would be appreciated and may include, but are not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports." (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2021 To 12/08/2025). Based on our initial review, we believe we cannot process your request within the statutory 20 workdays because our organization has a significant number of pending FOIA requests.  To ensure all requesters are properly served in the order their requests were received, we have instituted multitrack processing of requests.  Based on the information you provided, we have placed your request in the complex track. The Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category.  Based upon the information provided in your request, we have placed you in the Other category.  Regarding your request for a fee waiver, we defer regarding this, or any subsequent fee issue until the review of responsive records is complete.  At that time you will be contacted relative to fees. We have assigned number 2026-01591-F to identify your request; should you need to contact us about your request.  Please direct your questions to SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.Workflow@us.af.mil and use this assigned number to assist us in responding more promptly. We will strive to provide an update between 90 and 120 days.  Our policy is to process requests within their respective tracks in the order in which they were received in accordance with 32 CFR, Part 286.8.  We will notify you in writing if additional processing time is required.  We do process each FOIA request as quickly as possible, however delayed responses can be expected. Very Respectfully, SAF/AMGI FOIA TEAM Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000

communications.subject: Final Response - AF FOIA 2026-01591-F
communications.communication:
Dear Brett  Carollo, The attached letter is in final response to your December 8, 2025 Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for a list of all the films that the US Air Force has participated in the production of, in any capacity whatsoever, for the year 2021. Any and all such lists, full or partial, would be appreciated and may include, but are not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports. You requested a search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ. (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2021 To 12/08/2025). 
If you are dissatisfied with the servicing of this request or this final response from this office, you may contact the Air Force FOIA Public Liaison Officer, Mr. Robert Bivins, concerning this response at AF FOIA Public Liaison Office (SAF/CIO A6XA), 1800 Air Force Pentagon, Washington, DC 20330-1800, usaf.pentagon.saf-cio-a6.mbx.af-foia@mail.mil or (703) 614-8500.  Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. 

Please contact this office at the address above, via email at SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.Workflow@us.af.mil, should you have any questions and refer to case AF 2026-01591-F.  Department of Defense Regulation 5400.7/Air Force Manual 33-302 indicates fees may be assessed for processing this request; however, there are no applicable fees in this instance. 

Sincerely, 
SAF/AMGI FOIA TEAM Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000

title: WSP Officer Rosters

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WSP Officer Rosters
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request any electronic spreadsheets maintained by the Washington State Patrol that show the following for all current and former sworn employees of the WSP, year by year, dating back as far as data are maintained:

• Name
• Badge number(s)
• Employee ID
• CJTC ID number
• Date of hire
• Rank/job title
• Date of separation, if applicable
• Reason for separation, if applicable
• All assignments within WSP (e.g., office, section, other assignments) and dates of assignments/assignment changes

This request seeks electronic data under RCW 42.56.010.

Invisible Institute is an award-winning nonprofit public accountability journalism organization based in Chicago. Our mission is to enhance the capacity of citizens to hold public institutions accountable. Because I, the requestor, am a journalist, and I seek the information in order to disseminate it to the public, and because the information regards the safety, welfare, and legal rights of the general public, I request a fee waiver for this data under RCW 42.56.120(4). It is not for commercial benefit because I am a member of the nonprofit news media whose principal purpose is to access and disseminate information regarding the general public’s safety, welfare, and legal rights. To the extent you intend to assess any charges, please notify me to discuss first.

Thank you for your time and prompt attention to this matter. Please contact me should you have any questions related to this request.

Sam Stecklow
Invisible Institute
Experimental Station
6100 South Blackstone Avenue
Chicago, Illinois 60637

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Stecklow

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2106816/” Chapter 42.56 RCW governs disclosure of public records in Washington.  The Public Records Act is generally construed liberally to promote access to public records.  See RCW 42.56.030.  However, RCW 42.56.070(9) specifically prohibits the disclosure of "lists of individuals requested for commercial purposes".  This section "does not distinguish between different types of commercial purposes".  AGO 1998 No. 2, at 3; see also AGO 1975 No. 15. 

Your public records request is for a list of individuals, with additional information about each individual.  RCW 42.56.070(9) prohibits state agencies from providing “lists of individuals requested for commercial purposes.”   Prior to providing you with the information you requested, please complete and return the Declaration of Non-Commercial Purpose form loaded to the portal. 

In order to confirm that this request is not for commercial purposes, please also provide the purpose for which this list will be used.  “An agency has an obligation to investigate (and) at least require a party requesting a list of individuals to state the purpose of the request.”  (SEIU 775 v. DSHS and Freedom Foundation Div II 2016) 

Please log in to the Public Records Center at the below link to retrieve the Declaration. Please note that this link will only remain active for 30 days. Public Records Request - R015130-101325 Once your completed declaration is returned, we will be able to proceed with your request.  If the completed declaration is not received within 30 days, we will consider this request abandoned and close it.  Should you have any questions or to view the full details of this request please log into the Public Records Center.  Sincerely, Den Declet, Forms and Records Analyst 3

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Leon County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Leon County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe
