communications.communication:
January 29, 2026 
 RE: Public Records Request R036198-012926 
 
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe: 
This letter acknowledges receipt of your public records request dated January 29, 2026 for the following items: 
 Describe Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2201930/ 
 Type of Records Requested: Other 
 Type of Detention Records Requested: 
 Involved Party Detail: 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
If this list does not accurately reflect the item(s) you requested, please contact us immediately at (850) 606-3317 for clarification. To ensure a proper and timely response, your request will be processed in the order in which it was received. It is the policy of the Sheriff's Office to process all requests as quickly as possible. If you provided contact information, we will contact you when your request is completed. If you did not provide contact information, please call (850) 606-3317 for the status of your request. 
 NOTE: The Identity or name of sexual battery victims, child abuse victims, elder abuse victims, 911 callers, Marsy's Law claim, misdemeanor juvenile arrests, spouses and children of law enforcement officers, firefighters, and other statutorily protected occupations are exempt from release or confidential per Florida Statutes: sections 365.171(12), 394.4615(1), 985.04(1), 119.071(4), 119.071(2)(h) and 415.107(1). Thus, we are unable to provide records for these based on a name search, as doing so would identify them by name. 
If you should have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us at (850) 606-3317. Please reference Request Number R036198-012926 in all communications. 
 Please note: Under Florida's Public Records laws, most written communications to or from Sheriff's Office staff or officials regarding Sheriff's Office business are public records available to the public and media upon request. Your communications and/or 119 Public Records request may, therefore, be subject to public disclosure. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.

Good afternoon.  Our internal affairs unit has provided me with data from 2017 thru 2024.  However, it isn't consistent for each year.  Some are spread sheets and some are annual reports.  There have been three different programs for Response to Resistance reports during this time period.  I can provide this information, or I can start pulling the yearly reports.  I'm uncertain how many reports there are in total but there are fees associated with review and redaction.  We bill 100 pages per staff hour.  One staff hour is $28.38 per hour.  This could very well be hundreds of dollars.  Let me know if you wish to review the annual reports and datasheets that are available. title: Boulder County Flock ALPR

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request #R000116-011526
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. ALPR Locations: Please provide the precise location of each Flock Safety ALPR camera deployed and utilized by Boulder County Sheriff's Office.
2. Email Communications with Flock Safety (specifically emails sent to and received from email addresses ending in “@flocksafety.com”): All metadata(from address, to address(es), cc address(es), bcc address(es), date/time sent, date/time received, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, only the specified metadata.) of email communications between Boulder County Sheriff's Office and Flock Safety between January 1, 2021 and the present.
3. Hotlist Criteria: Please provide documentation of policies detailing the criteria and procedures for creating and verifying entries on hotlists in Flock Safety's ALPR system. Specifically:
a. Criteria for adding entries to hotlists (e.g., offense types, jurisdictional warrants, etc.)
b. Verification and approval processes before hotlist alerts are actionable
4. Data-Sharing Agreements: List of all agencies with whom Boulder County Sheriff's Office shares ALPR data, both within Colorado and out-of-state, including federal entities.
5. Policies on ALPR Use and Oversight: Any formal policies or guidelines related to:
a. Acceptable and prohibited uses of ALPR data, including specific restrictions and permissible use cases
b. Access policy, including what constitutes a “valid reason” for system access
c. Human verification procedures for hotlist alerts before law enforcement action
6. Access Logs and Audits: Any access logs, audits, compliance reports, or oversight records evaluating the use, accuracy, and policy compliance of Boulder County Sheriff's Office ALPR systems between January 1, 2021 and the present.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Spratlin

communications.subject: Records Request :: R000116-011526
communications.communication:
Dear MATTHEW SPRATLIN: 
We appreciate your interest in criminal justice records of the Boulder County Sheriff’s Office. Your request was received on  1/15/2026 and will be processed according to the Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act (CCJRA), C.R.S. 24-72-301 et seq., and other applicable laws. Disclosure of records involving children may be restricted by the Children’s Code Records and Information Act, C.R.S. 19-1-301 et seq. 
The Sheriff’s Office Records Section does not process requests for public records under the Colorado Open Records Act (CORA), C.R.S. 24-72-201 et seq. Requests for public records under CORA should be submitted to the Boulder County’s Open Records Center available at https://bouldercounty.gov/records/colorado-open-records-act/. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXaWhAOeQS4p-2FTifjFiHmIBToUhCNwPrijCljaecY3mINRUTFikS2djaQyg-2FJ7hIZ8luPa63HTgmuk3H1tWjGbKI-3DQW5S_Y6hkOFzc7Gmm3-2Bi7zo-2BZvuu4uIOBSkIp7jMmXKFRuYHJRlwdqXH86OpLg0WOws0MTIxkdrrxCuNEvvOPj2QMFlM-2FT-2FZ7glL-2BBqYg9N4wYHZ8lWuxVeAa-2B7P3ulgRA8fuExOEDO32UGNLt8gJiNNi-2FTmlOXJMMM5C6bP7PaJw-2F7l8wABoXl3wkVYCMFqPMD8UbKoFrnP9oUF-2FgVIN352e055NnrnUqbc7SpPbnSvla8Q-3D) 
You will receive an email when the response to your request is ready. While the Records Section is working diligently to respond, please note that the 3-day timeframe for inspection under CORA does not apply to requests for criminal justice records under the CCJRA. This request can be tracked at the link below using assigned reference number R000116-011526. 
The Sheriff’s Office also publishes certain criminal justice information online, including daily booking and listing reports for the Boulder County Jail, available at: https://bouldercounty.gov/safety/jail/listing-and-booking-reports/. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXaWhAOeQS4p-2FTifjFiHmIBT2MJBJQmvdwUDiv0zYVq10kivwqXhRz5ZRo0NzoQrJNkh6yFBa-2FP7KQLm7eFSs948-3DpP8T_Y6hkOFzc7Gmm3-2Bi7zo-2BZvuu4uIOBSkIp7jMmXKFRuYHJRlwdqXH86OpLg0WOws0MTIxkdrrxCuNEvvOPj2QMFmKLNulZn-2FRuB2-2FuXNkTTOzLJFJlIucpmrnUfewSM76Sk5iGKQX7kfEvKZzeodUz-2B28lQGCUaemLEQi91br7cP1GeS8gScJHiYoQgC9C-2FbVNq9vqZJ2XXtctyOajwSoXHsSeZs8Sfzf9ox88G5zqe3A-3D)

Boulder County Sheriff’s Office

communications.subject: CORA Request :: C000035-012126
communications.communication:
Dear Matthew Spratlin: 
Thank you for your interest in the public information of Boulder County. Your request was received on 1/21/2026 and given the reference number C000035-012126 for tracking purposes. 
Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. ALPR Locations: Please provide the precise location of each Flock Safety ALPR camera deployed and utilized by Boulder County Sheriff's Office. 
2. Email Communications with Flock Safety (specifically emails sent to and received from email addresses ending in “@flocksafety.com”): All metadata(from address, to address(es), cc address(es), bcc address(es), date/time sent, date/time received, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, only the specified metadata.) of email communications between Boulder County Sheriff's Office and Flock Safety between January 1, 2021 and the present. 
3. Hotlist Criteria: Please provide documentation of policies detailing the criteria and procedures for creating and verifying entries on hotlists in Flock Safety's ALPR system. Specifically: 
a. Criteria for adding entries to hotlists (e.g., offense types, jurisdictional warrants, etc.) 
b. Verification and approval processes before hotlist alerts are actionable 
4. Data-Sharing Agreements: List of all agencies with whom Boulder County Sheriff's Office shares ALPR data, both within Colorado and out-of-state, including federal entities. 
5. Policies on ALPR Use and Oversight: Any formal policies or guidelines related to: 
a. Acceptable and prohibited uses of ALPR data, including specific restrictions and permissible use cases 
b. Access policy, including what constitutes a “valid reason” for system access 
c. Human verification procedures for hotlist alerts before law enforcement action 
6. Access Logs and Audits: Any access logs, audits, compliance reports, or oversight records evaluating the use, accuracy, and policy compliance of Boulder County Sheriff's Office ALPR systems between January 1, 2021 and the present. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Matthew Spratlin 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2179756/ 
You will be contacted about the availability of records and/or provided with copies of the records. Depending on the scope of your request, a deposit and/or extension may be requested. If a deposit and/or extension is needed, Boulder County will contact you with an estimate before proceeding. All communications will be through the Boulder County Records Center. PLEASE NOTE: The Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) does not require Boulder County to create new records, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request under " My Requests" in the Boulder County Open Records Center.

communications.subject: BCSO Records Request :: R000116-011526
communications.communication:

Dear MATTHEW SPRATLIN: Our agency's records request numbers begin with R, so we don't have any information on the request with the reference #C00035-012126.  Is it possible this is referencing a request you made through Boulder County's Open Records Center?  This is the link to their request page: https://bouldercounty.gov/records/colorado-open-records-act/ You are correct that this request (#R000116-011526) through Boulder County Sheriff's Office is still open & pending.  We are currently drafting a response to this request, and when it is complete you will receive a link via email.  The link will connect you back to this portal to view the response. Thank you, Boulder County Sheriff's Office

title: Missing Person Jonathan Hoang (Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Missing Person Jonathan Hoang (Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Given the case of missing person Jonathan Hoang (Case Number S025-42596) has been determined "no evidence of foul play" and therefore, not under criminal investigation, the following documents are requested for public review to aid in the search to find this vulnerable young man:
1. Any incident report, narrative, or similar report concerning the endangered missing persons case of Jonathan Hoang.
2. Any police or investigator notes, including interviews with neighbors, school teachers/administrators, or family members.
3. Any notes, memos or meeting recordings or communications within and across law enforcement, federal, or government agencies (including communications with Kirkland Police Department, WA state FBI, WA state senators) related to the Jonathan Hoang case.
4. Any recordings: bodycam footage, vehical camera footage, video recordings related to the Jonathan Hoang case. 
5. Any notes, memos or meeting recordings related to communications with the Hoang family.
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Elin Bjorling

title: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

In light of recent public reporting and video documentation showing federal immigration personnel operating vehicles in the Twin Cities without visible license plates, with expired registration tabs, or in apparent violation of Minnesota vehicle statutes, I am seeking data to better understand how your agency enforces these laws uniformly within its jurisdiction, including whether such statutes are applied consistently regardless of the driver’s federal affiliation. The information we are requesting is not available on your Community Crime Map. We are requesting:

1. Summary Enforcement Data

Please provide a monthly summary (January 1, 2025 through present) of citations and warnings issued for the following violation types:
- Expired registration tabs
- No license plates
- Improperly displayed plates
- Switched or fictitious plates
- Operating a vehicle without valid registration

These may include (but are not limited to) the following statutes: Minn. Stat. § 168.09 – Registration / Display of Registration Plates, Minn. Stat. § 168.04 – Display of License Plates, Minn. Stat. § 169.79 – License Plates; Display Requirements, Minn. Stat. § 168.12 – Registration Tax / Tabs

For each statute listed above, please provide:
- Total citations issued (monthly totals)
- Total warnings issued (monthly totals - if available)
- Whether citation resulted in arrest (Y/N summary)
- Any internal code used for these violations
- Please do NOT requesting any personally identifiable information
----------

2. Stops or Citations Involving Federal Law Enforcement Personnel

For the same date range, please provide:
- The number of traffic stops conducted by Hopkins PD involving drivers identified as federal law enforcement personnel (if tracked)
- The number of citations or warnings issued to federal law enforcement personnel for the statutes listed above
- The number of such stops that resulted in no citation or warning
- If identity of agency (e.g., DHS, ICE, FBI) is tracked in any field, please include aggregate totals by agency.
- No names or personal identifying data are requested.

If no responsive records exist, please confirm that fact.
----------

3. Policies and Guidance

Please provide copies of:
- Current Hopkins PD written policies regarding enforcement of vehicle registration and license plate violations.
- Any written policy, directive, or standing order addressing enforcement discretion for government-owned or federal vehicles.
- Any temporary directives or operational guidance issued during Operation Metro Surge that address traffic enforcement priorities. This may include patrol briefs, SOPs, or other temporary directives issued regarding enforcement of plate/tab laws. Please include any memos, emails, powerpoint slides, or other documentation regarding temporary guidance for these vehicle violations since Operation Metro Surge began. 

Thank you in advance for the assistance. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon. Let me know if I can provide additional context to aid in your search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: FW: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD

Hello,



Pursuant to your request, please see attached sheet of all citations and warnings issued from 1/1/2025 - 3/1/2026.

1. Summary Enforcement Data

- Expired registration tabs - see attached spreadsheet of data
- No license plates - see attached spreadsheet of data
- Improperly displayed plates- see attached spreadsheet of data
- Switched or fictitious plates- see attached spreadsheet of data
- Operating a vehicle without valid registration- see attached spreadsheet of data

These may include (but are not limited to) the following statutes: Minn. Stat. § 168.09 - Registration / Display of Registration Plates, Minn. Stat. § 168.04 - Display of License Plates, Minn. Stat. § 169.79 - License Plates; Display Requirements, Minn. Stat. § 168.12 - Registration Tax / Tabs

For each statute listed above, please provide:
- Total citations issued (monthly totals) - see attached spreadsheet of data
- Total warnings issued (monthly totals - if available) - see attached spreadsheet of data
- Whether citation resulted in arrest (Y/N summary) - this data is not collected
- Any internal code used for these violations - not applicable, we use no internal codes
- Please do NOT requesting any personally identifiable information

2. Stops or Citations Involving Federal Law Enforcement Personnel

For the same date range, please provide:

- If identity of agency (e.g., DHS, ICE, FBI) is tracked in any field, please include aggregate totals by agency. - not tracked
- No names or personal identifying data are requested.

If no responsive records exist, please confirm that fact. - no records exist

3. Policies and Guidance

Please provide copies of: - You can view our policy manual here. Police-Department-Policy-PDF<https://www.hopkinsmn.com/DocumentCenter/View/595/Police-Department-Policy-PDF>   We did not issue any temporary directives or policies pertaining to Operation Metro Surge.
- Current Hopkins PD written policies regarding enforcement of vehicle registration and license plate violations.
- Any written policy, directive, or standing order addressing enforcement discretion for government-owned or federal vehicles.
- Any temporary directives or operational guidance issued during Operation Metro Surge that address traffic enforcement priorities. This may include patrol briefs, SOPs, or other temporary directives issued regarding enforcement of plate/tab laws. Please include any memos, emails, powerpoint slides, or other documentation regarding temporary guidance for these vehicle violations since Operation Metro Surge began.





Thank you,
